name: 1. Exact path
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/health
identity:
  name: bob

name: 2. Multiple exact paths
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/status
identity:
  name: bob

name: 3. Greedy glob
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/users/123
identity:
  name: bob

name: 4. Glob + capture + contains
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /data/x/b/info
identity:
  name: bob

name: 5. Method
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/users
identity:
  name: bob

name: 6. Methods
input:
request:
  method: HEAD
  path: /api/users
identity:
  name: bob

name: 7. Capture checked against trait
rule:
app_resources:
  - paths: ["/api/v4/projects/{project}/**"]
    methods: [GET]
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/projects/alpha/issues
identity:
  name: alice
  traits:
    allowed_projects: [alpha, beta]

name: 8. Capture equals identity
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/user/alice
identity:
  name: alice

name: 9. Role required
input:
request:
  method: DELETE
  path: /admin/settings
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [admin]

name: 10. Carve-out (negation)
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/users
identity:
  name: bob

name: 11. where holds the whole predicate
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /metrics
identity:
  name: bob

name: 12. Additive list (two rules)
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /static/css/app.css
identity:
  name: bob

name: 13. Denied: wrong method
input:
request:
  method: POST
  path: /api/users
identity:
  name: bob

name: 14. Anti-pattern (commented out)
rule:
app_resources:
  # When one rule lists several paths, bind the same captures in each.
  # The second path below binds {team}, not {project}, so a request that
  # matches it leaves vars.project unbound and the rule denies, fail-closed.
  # Shown commented out so it does not run:
  #
  # - paths: ["/api/v4/projects/{project}/**", "/api/v4/teams/{team}/**"]
  #   where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
  - paths: ["/api/v4/projects/{project}/**"]
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/projects/alpha/issues
identity:
  name: alice
  traits:
    allowed_projects: [alpha, beta]
